search_queries: 2026 IRS Publication 590-B required minimum distributions start age table
IRS Publication 590-B 2026 relief years 10-year rule required minimum distributions
IRS Publication 590-B eligible designated beneficiary classes required minimum distributions
contract_notes: This entry covers the public distribution_rules lookup payload only. Use the nested contract groups required_beginning, account_applicability, and beneficiary_distribution exactly as shown in the prompt pack.

In required_beginning, represent still_working_exception with both eligible_plan_categories and eligible_account_types. Keep the 1959 birth-year rule as its own start_age_rules entry when the source distinguishes it, and carry any interim-guidance nuance in guidance_status and notes rather than flattening it away.

This public contract is year-bucketed for current-year operational RMD rules. If an IRS source repeats the legacy pre-July-1-1949 age-70 1/2 cutoff, capture that nuance in the first start_age_rules notes field rather than marking schema_change_required solely because the public contract does not split that legacy cohort into a separate row.

In account_applicability, list account types according to the rule for the requested year. For 2026, designated_roth_plan_account belongs in owner_exempt_account_types, not owner_required_account_types. Use designated_roth_owner_exemption_effective_year to record the SECURE 2.0 owner-lifetime exemption effective year.

In beneficiary_distribution, distinguish beneficiary_categories, recognized_beneficiary_classes, eligible_designated_beneficiary_classes, life_expectancy_method_by_class, ten_year_rule, and non_designated_beneficiary_rules. Do not collapse eligible designated beneficiary subclasses into top-level beneficiary categories.
The uniform_lifetime_table, single_life_table, and joint_life_table are maintained as separate keys and should not be duplicated in this pipeline output.

Do not mark schema_change_required merely because the underlying Rust function stores ten_year_rule, relief_years, or pre_1987_403b fields outside the nested public lookup contract. Those internal fields are expected and should be mapped into the public shape.
